mission.title: argo-events: Azure Event Hub Trigger
mission.description: Azure Event Hub Trigger. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-events deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-events version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
helm list -n argo-events 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-events installation.

steps.title: Review argo-events configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-events configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl get configmap -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-events
```
There doesn't seem to be a trigger dedicated for Azure Event Hubs. Though you can use a Kafka Sensor to use Azure Event Hubs (since you can deploy a managed Kafka in Azure Event Hubs), it would still be nice to have this integration.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Azure Event Hub Trigger
steps.description:
Checklist:

Test Cases:

Notes:
- parametrization will not work for `fqdn` and `hubName` parameters since the Azure Event Hubs Client is initialized in the `NewAzureEventHubsTrigger` function. Should this be the case, I'll need to add code that does this (but might be a bit tricky since these values are required to initialize the client. Suggestions are welcome on how to solve this
- advanced features for Azure Events Hub such as sending with a message ID or sending to a specific

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl get events -n argo-events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Azure Event Hub Trigger" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Checklist:

Test Cases:

Notes:
- parametrization will not work for `fqdn` and `hubName` parameters since the Azure Event Hubs Client is initialized in the `NewAzureEventHubsTrigger` function. Should this be the case, I'll need to add code that does this (but might be a bit tricky since these values are required to initialize the client.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-events installed or the issue environment reproducible.
